A curated directory of human-written websites, blogs, and corners of the internet worth visiting

import { Card, CardGrid, Aside } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';

This site is a curated collection of **human-written websites, blogs, and corners of the internet worth visiting**.

No AI sludge.  
No recycled SEO filler.  
No empty content farms pretending to be useful.

Just real people writing real things.

<Aside type="tip" title="What this is">
This is not trying to be the biggest directory on the internet.

It is trying to be a **good one**.
</Aside>

---

## What makes it different

<CardGrid stagger>
  <Card title="Found by hand" icon="search">
    Every site listed here has been found manually, checked manually, and added because it actually feels worth your time.
  </Card>

  <Card title="Picked with taste" icon="check">
    This is not a giant dump of links. The goal is to keep the quality high and the browsing experience clean.
  </Card>

  <Card title="Built for discovery" icon="open-book">
    The site is organised so you can actually browse properly — by theme, mood, and interest — instead of endlessly scrolling through noise.
  </Card>

  <Card title="Human first" icon="heart">
    If a site feels lifeless, mass-produced, or machine-written, it does not belong here.
  </Card>
</CardGrid>

---

## What you’ll find here

<CardGrid>
  <Card title="Personal writing" icon="document">
    Blogs with voice, personality, reflection, and lived experience.
  </Card>

  <Card title="Culture & places" icon="map-pin">
    Place-based writing, local voices, history, landscapes, and life rooted somewhere real.
  </Card>

  <Card title="Travel" icon="paper-plane-tilt">
    Travel blogs and journeys with substance, not just affiliate fluff.
  </Card>

  <Card title="Tech & development" icon="setting">
    Thoughtful technical writing, indie developers, web people, and builder blogs.
  </Card>

  <Card title="Communities" icon="users">
    Small forums, niche communities, and internet spaces still worth visiting.
  </Card>

  <Card title="Tools & hosting" icon="window">
    Platforms, hosting, and website tools used by bloggers and independent sites.
  </Card>
</CardGrid>

---

## Why this exists

The modern web is full of things that look useful for about three seconds:

- AI-written articles with no point of view  
- affiliate pages pretending to be recommendations  
- copy-and-paste content written to rank, not to say anything  
- websites with traffic goals but no soul  

This site exists because the web is better when you can still stumble across:

- a weird personal blog  
- a thoughtful essay  
- a niche site made by someone who clearly cares  
- a page that feels like a person, not a strategy  

<Aside type="caution" title="What you won’t find here">
This site is not for AI-generated content, thin affiliate sites, generic SEO blogs, or mass-produced junk with nothing to say.
</Aside>

---

## How sites get added

There’s no automation here.

Every site is:

- found manually  
- checked manually  
- placed in a category that fits  
- given a short description by hand  

That means the list grows slower. That’s deliberate.

<Aside type="tip" title="The rule">
If it doesn’t feel human, it doesn’t go on the site.
</Aside>

---

## Built simply

The site is intentionally lightweight.

<CardGrid>
  <Card title="Astro" icon="rocket">
    Static, fast, and ideal for a content-first site like this.
  </Card>

  <Card title="Starlight" icon="star">
    Gives the site a clean structure that is easy to browse and maintain.
  </Card>

  <Card title="Markdown" icon="pencil">
    Entries are written manually, which keeps everything simple and portable.
  </Card>

  <Card title="No bloat" icon="close">
    No database. No needless complexity. No tracking nonsense.
  </Card>
</CardGrid>

---

## Want to suggest a site?

If you know a site worth adding, send it over.

Good fits:

- personal blogs  
- niche websites  
- independent creators  
- thoughtful small web projects  
- communities with real life in them  

Not a fit:

- AI content mills  
- parasite SEO pages  
- thin affiliate sites  
- generic “10 best” sludge  

---

## Final note

This site is not about building the largest list.

It is about building a list that still feels like it was made by a person.

And that matters.

## Why this exists

The internet used to feel more personal — like wandering through people’s thoughts rather than scrolling through noise.

That feeling is disappearing.

This site is an attempt to bring a small part of it back.

<CardGrid>
	<Card title="Curated, not automated" icon="check">
		Every link is added by hand. If it’s here, it’s because it feels worth your time.
	</Card>
	<Card title="No dead internet sludge" icon="close">
		No AI spam. No empty SEO pages. No mass-produced filler pretending to be useful.
	</Card>
	<Card title="Built for discovery" icon="compass">
		The goal is not to overwhelm you with links — it’s to help you find something genuinely worth reading.
	</Card>
</CardGrid>

<Aside type="caution" title="What you won’t find here">
This is not a giant dump of backlinks, scraped content, or AI-generated junk. If it doesn’t feel human, it doesn’t belong here.
</Aside>

Here you can:

- discover voices you’ve never heard before

- browse by mood, topic, or curiosity  
- reconnect with the human side of the web

Hosting services used to run blogs, websites, and self-hosted platforms
Platforms and tools used to create and publish blogs

Tools and platforms people use to write and publish blogs — from simple hosted options to fully self-hosted setups. --- ### 🧱 Hosted Platforms (Easy to Start) * WordPress.com – Hosted version of WordPress, easy to get started. * Ghost – Clean, modern publishing platform focused on writing. * Substack – Newsletter-style blogging with built-in audience tools. * Medium – Large publishing platform with built-in readership. * Write.as – Minimal, privacy-focused writing platform. --- ### 🧠 Self-Hosted Platforms (More Control) * WordPress.org – Full control, plugins, themes, and flexibility. * Ghost (Self-Hosted) – Fast, modern, and focused on content. * Grav – Flat-file CMS, no database required. * Jekyll – Static site generator used with GitHub Pages. * Hugo – Fast static site generator for blogs and sites. --- ### ⚡ Modern & Developer-Friendly * Astro – Static-first framework (what this site uses 👀). * Next.js – React framework for building fast websites. * Nuxt – Vue-based framework for modern web apps. * SvelteKit – Lightweight framework for fast web apps. --- ### 📝 Minimal & Indie Platforms * Bear Blog – Simple, no-nonsense blogging platform. * Micro.blog – Indie blogging and micro-posting platform. * Blot – Turns a folder into a blog. * Listed – Blogging powered by Standard Notes. --- ### Why these platforms? These are tools used by real people writing real blogs — not content farms or mass-produced sites. Some are simple, some are powerful, but all of them allow people to publish their own voice on the web.
